The Quiet Restructuring of Big Tech

By Sam Lee

For most of the past two years, the major US technology companies have run something between a hiring freeze and a slow contraction. The headcount numbers, though, do not tell the whole story. Behind the public reorganisations and the quarterly layoff announcements is a deeper structural shift in how these companies are organised — one that, when it is finished, will look less like the platform-era org charts we grew up with and more like the diversified industrial conglomerates of the 1970s.
